one_liner: Statistical selective inference to correct cherry-picked validation in medical AI benchmarking
problem: Medical AI papers routinely select the best run across hyperparameter sweeps; classical p-values are invalid post-selection. Selective inference (Lee 2016) provides valid post-selection confidence intervals.
approach: 1.Reproduce 3-5 UQ methods on ISIC/BraTS recording full hyperparameter search trajectories. 2.Apply selective inference to correct confidence intervals. 3.Quantify empirical deflation factor vs naive p-values.
why_new: Selective inference (Lee 2016) standard in GWAS; only a few ML papers (Zrnic 2023) apply it; medical imaging AI has zero applications. Classic debunking story with stable fallback venue.
source_method: statistical selective inference (Lee et al. 2016 polyhedral lemma; Zrnic and Candes 2023)

one_liner: Bayesian experimental design BALD to MedSAM prompt-position active learning
problem: SAM/MedSAM interactive segmentation requires extensive prompt labeling; nobody has embedded BALD into the prompt optimization loop wasting annotation budget on low-information samples.
approach: 1.Attach MC-Dropout head to MedSAM use BALD score to select next prompt position. 2.Evaluate Dice retention at 50% and 25% annotation budgets on BraTS and Camelyon16. 3.Compare against random and entropy acquisition.
why_new: BALD (Houlsby 2011, Gal 2017) exists for natural image segmentation but embedding in SAM prompt loop is new; existing MedSAM active learning selects images not prompt positions.
source_method: Bayesian experimental design BALD (Houlsby et al. 2011; Gal et al. 2017 deep Bayesian AL)

one_liner: RL UCB exploration bonus to dynamic threshold design for medical selective prediction
problem: Selective prediction rejection thresholds are globally fixed and cannot adapt to incoming distribution; RL UCB/Thompson dynamic scheduling has never been applied to online abstention decisions.
approach: 1.Formalize selective prediction as contextual bandit accept=arm1 abstain=arm2 reward=accuracy target. 2.Run UCB adaptive threshold on HAM10000/BraTS vs fixed threshold and Geifman 2017 SelectiveNet. 3.Analyze convergence vs coverage.
why_new: Contextual bandit for selective prediction just appeared in NLP (Ye 2024); medical imaging is blank. Core distinction: dynamic vs static threshold.
source_method: RL contextual bandit UCB-Thompson (Auer 2002; Russo and Van Roy 2016)

one_liner: Diffusion model score function variance as single-pass medical reconstruction quality warning
problem: Diffusion models increasingly used for MRI/CT reconstruction but score function variance not used as uncertainty signal; existing methods require expensive multi-sample approaches with no single-pass UQ.
approach: 1.Extract score function gradient norm at DDIM denoising steps as per-pixel uncertainty proxy. 2.Validate correlation between score-variance and reconstruction error on fastMRI and BraTS FLAIR. 3.Compare efficiency vs MC-ensemble reference upper bound.
why_new: Score function variance has theoretical grounding (Ho 2020, Song 2021) but never used for medical reconstruction quality warning. WACV 2025 pixel-wise aleatoric uses multi-step sampling; this targets single-pass.
source_method: diffusion score function (Song and Ermon 2020; Ho 2020 DDPM; DDIM Song 2021)
